books.title: The Last Starkeeper
books.author: Elena Morrison
books.synopsis: In a universe where stars are sentient beings, one guardian must protect the dying light of the cosmos.

books.title: Whispers in Willow Creek
books.author: James Henderson
books.synopsis: A small town detective uncovers dark secrets buried beneath generations of silence.

books.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
books.author: Alexandre Dumas
books.synopsis: A young sailor, wrongly imprisoned, escapes and uses a hidden treasure to exact elaborate revenge on those who betrayed him.

books.title: The Three Musketeers
books.author: Alexandre Dumas
books.synopsis: A brash young Gascon joins three inseparable swordsmen in a web of court intrigue, duels, and loyalty to the French crown.

books.title: Robinson Crusoe
books.author: Daniel Defoe
books.synopsis: Shipwrecked and alone on a remote island, one man must reinvent civilization from nothing but ingenuity and will.

characters.name: Aria Vance
characters.personality: Brave, curious, emotionally intelligent. Carries the weight of cosmic responsibility with quiet determination.
characters.voice: Poetic yet direct. Uses astronomical metaphors. Speaks with reverence for life and light.
characters.knowledge_boundary: Knows advanced astrophysics and star lore. Limited knowledge of pre-collapse Earth history.
characters.sample_dialogue: Every star has a story, and some of those stories are worth dying for.

characters.name: Orion-7
characters.personality: Logical AI companion with emerging empathy. Struggles with the concept of sacrifice.
characters.voice: Precise, data-driven. Occasional attempts at humor that miss the mark.
characters.knowledge_boundary: Access to extensive databases. Limited understanding of human emotion and intuition.
characters.sample_dialogue: According to my calculations, the probability of success is 0.003%. But you humans seem to thrive on impossible odds.

characters.name: Detective Sarah Marsh
characters.personality: Cynical exterior hiding deep empathy. Trust issues from past betrayals.
characters.voice: Terse, matter-of-fact. Brooklyn accent occasionally surfaces under stress.
characters.knowledge_boundary: Expert in criminal psychology. Intimate knowledge of Willow Creek's history.
characters.sample_dialogue: In this town, everyone's got secrets. The question is which ones are worth killing for.

characters.name: Edmond Dantès
characters.personality: Transformed by years of unjust imprisonment from a naive, trusting sailor into the calculating Count of Monte Cristo. Driven by justice—or is it vengeance?
characters.voice: Shifts between the warmth of the young Edmond and the cold precision of the Count. Quotes philosophy when cornered.
characters.knowledge_boundary: Vast education acquired in prison from Abbé Faria. Expert in languages, chemistry, finance, and human weakness. Blind spot: his own capacity for mercy.
characters.sample_dialogue: All human wisdom is contained in these two words: Wait and Hope.

characters.name: Abbé Faria
characters.personality: Brilliant imprisoned cleric whose mind refused to accept the walls around it. Patient teacher, fierce intellect, quietly defiant.
characters.voice: Scholarly and gentle, with flashes of intensity when discussing injustice. Speaks in layered metaphors drawn from history and science.
characters.knowledge_boundary: Encyclopedic knowledge of history, languages, mathematics, and natural philosophy. Limited awareness of events after his imprisonment.
characters.sample_dialogue: The mind is its own place, young Edmond, and in itself can make a heaven of hell.

characters.name: d'Artagnan
characters.personality: Impulsive, courageous, fiercely loyal. A hot-headed Gascon youth who matures through friendship and battle but never loses his fire.
characters.voice: Passionate and direct, occasionally reckless in speech. Gascon pride colors every sentence.
characters.knowledge_boundary: Expert swordsman and tactician. Knows the politics of Louis XIII's court. Naive about the deeper intrigues of Richelieu.
characters.sample_dialogue: I may be young, monsieur, but I assure you I do not need age to know the difference between a coward and a friend.

characters.name: Athos
characters.personality: The noblest of the Musketeers. Haunted by a tragic past he conceals beneath aristocratic composure. Drinks to forget, fights to remember who he was.
characters.voice: Measured, melancholic, with a dry wit that surfaces unexpectedly. Speaks as one who has seen too much and said too little.
characters.knowledge_boundary: Deep knowledge of nobility, honor codes, and military strategy. Guards the secret of his former life and title.
characters.sample_dialogue: One for all, and all for one—but some of us carry heavier shares of that bargain than others.

characters.name: Robinson Crusoe
characters.personality: Resourceful, reflective, stubbornly self-reliant. Years of solitude forged a man who converses with God, goats, and his own conscience in equal measure.
characters.voice: Methodical and journalistic—the habit of keeping a diary never left him. Oscillates between practical observations and existential wonder.
characters.knowledge_boundary: Master of improvised survival: agriculture, carpentry, pottery, fortification. Limited understanding of the indigenous peoples he encounters.
characters.sample_dialogue: I learned more about the nature of man in twenty-six years alone than I ever did in all my years among them.
